objective: Decide whether qrel or gate relaxation can unblock the Lance R2 production cutover.

metric_scenarios.interpretation: This is the current same-query cutover gate; it fails.
Switching the headline category macro from primary nDCG to graded nDCG still fails.
A micro-average primary gate would still fail.
A micro-average graded gate would still fail.
A language-axis graded macro gate would still fail, with the largest remaining weakness in Korean/English buckets.

notes: Most top-1 misses are wrong-document or wrong-category retrievals, not merely missing acceptable_paths.
The HikariCP case already has the top-1 document listed as acceptable, but the primary gate remains strict by design.
Three direct diagnostic rank-1 cases were explained by running the diagnostic without reranker; the no-reranker same-query report made the overall gate worse.

decision.reason: The reasonable graded-nDCG relaxations still trail legacy by about 0.052 category macro and 0.096 micro. Qrel-only acceptable-path edits would not fix the current primary gate, and reranker removal worsens quality.
decision.recommended_next_step: Do not change the gate just to cut over. Continue Option A with stronger document-level structure, or request explicit product approval for a weaker gate or risky cutover.
